metaBuilds.title: Magical Bruiser (Mixed-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 7,200 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Bruiser (Physical-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 24 — about 1,794 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +48% more extended fights vs bruisers: try Spectral Schematics over Magnify (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 956 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +50% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Magnify (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 22% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 33% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 33% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 37% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 33% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Shinbi's main threat is Line Tempo; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 24% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
